3.why: Sorting lets two pointers converge correctly from both ends.
3.invariant: From here on, nums is non-decreasing.

4.why: We need the array length to bound the loops.

5.happening: Start an empty result list res.
5.why: It collects every quadruplet that sums to the target.
5.invariant: res holds all answers found so far.

6.why: Every quadruplet contains exactly one first element.
6.invariant: i scans the array left to right.

7.why: With i and j fixed, two pointers find the remaining pair.
7.invariant: j always stays to the right of i.

8.invariant: lo is the low end of the search window.

9.why: hi starts at the last element.

10.happening: lo ({lo}) < hi ({hi}) — keep searching this window.
10.why: There are still pairs to check between the two pointers.

10.happening: lo ({lo}) is no longer < hi ({hi}) — close this window.
10.why: The window is empty; advance to the next j.
10.invariant: Every pair for this i, j has been checked.

11.why: Compare this sum against the target to decide the next move.
11.invariant: s is the current quadruplet's sum.

12.why: These four numbers are a valid quadruplet.
12.invariant: Equality means we record this answer.

12.why: Not a match — decide which pointer to move.
12.invariant: We will narrow the window next.

13.why: It sums exactly to the target.
13.invariant: Adding it to the result set.

14.why: After a match, shrink from the left to seek new pairs.

15.why: After a match, also shrink from the right.

16.why: Move lo right to increase the sum.

16.happening: s ({s}) is not less than target ({target}) — too big.
16.why: Move hi left to decrease the sum.

17.happening: Sum too small → advance lo right (currently {lo}).
17.why: A larger left value raises the sum toward the target.

19.happening: Sum too big → retreat hi left (currently {hi}).
19.why: A smaller right value lowers the sum toward the target.

20.why: Every valid combination has been found.

init.why: Set up the variables the algorithm needs.
